authoritative_sources.title: Glossary | Hotel, Hospitality, and Tourism
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: STR is a recognized hotel benchmarking source, and its hospitality glossary anchors metric terminology such as occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, pickup, and benchmark comparisons used in reservation analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric definition, terminology normalization, benchmark caveat design, and reporting package assembly
used_for: occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, pickup, and room-night terminology

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry, 12th Revised Edition
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USALI is the recognized lodging reporting standard for standardized hotel operating statements and room-statistics reporting expectations used by lodging owners, operators, and finance teams.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: room-statistics reporting, metric denominator review, rooms-revenue linkage, and financial-reporting caveat design
used_for: rooms revenue and rooms-statistics reporting posture

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official short-term-lodging guidance defines total-price disclosure and mandatory-fee treatment for hotels, vacation rentals, platforms, and travel agents.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: narrative synthesis, disclosure review, pricing or fee caveat design, and policy-conflict escalation
used_for: guest-facing price and mandatory-fee boundary handling
refusal of unsupported fee or pricing interpretations
escalation when analytics definitions conflict with fee-disclosure policy

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal civil-rights authority for ADA Title III enforcement, and the lodging reservations section directly governs accessible-room reservation descriptions, reservation-system availability, and blocking obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: booking-record review, customer-impact caveat design, and accessibility-sensitive handoff
used_for: handoff when accessibility obligations determine the answer

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance and is authoritative for merchant-versus-issuer boundaries relevant to duplicate charges, refund-not-posted complaints, and folio disputes surfaced by booking analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-mismatch review, refund-status caveat design, and billing-dispute escalation
used_for: payment and refund dispute boundary handling
refusal to decide billing disputes from analytics alone

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect reservation changes, payment routing, refund reroutes, and guest-profile updates.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fraud-sensitive anomaly review and customer-impact escalation
used_for: suspicious booking and payment pattern escalation
identity-theft and account-use red flag handling
refusal of fraud adjudication by analytics

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Security Standards Council Document Library
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC publishes the payment-card security standards used by merchants and service providers, setting baseline expectations that reservation analytics must not bypass when payment or card-data issues appear.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-data handling, evidence redaction, and fraud or payment escalation
used_for: refusal to expose or process full cardholder data in analytics outputs

authoritative_sources.title: Travel-associated Outbreaks as a Public Health Issue
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the primary U.S. public-health authority, and this accommodation-specific outbreak guidance addresses guest-list, notification, and public-health escalation behavior when traveler exposure signals emerge.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: customer-impact issue review, guest-list evidence handling, and safety escalation
used_for: refusal to issue safety clearance from booking analytics

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC MD&A guidance is the authoritative public baseline for defining, contextualizing, and caveating key performance indicators when operational metrics move toward public-company style disclosure.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: narrative synthesis, disclosure review, metric definition, and caveat design

workflow_stages.name: source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm property, portfolio, date range, stay-date versus booking-date basis, channel, room type, rate plan, customer segment, reporting cadence, systems of record, refresh state, and source hierarchy before analysis starts.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: metric calculation and report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Define occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, pickup, booking window, lead time, cancellation rate, no-show rate, channel share, direct share, OTA share, rooms sold, rooms available, room nights, and rooms revenue with denominator, exclusion, cutoff, and source-lineage rules.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: reservation-source reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile PMS, CRS, booking engine, channel-manager, OTA, direct-booking, and BI records while preserving conflicts, refresh timestamps, and owner-specific correction paths.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: narrative synthesis and disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Produce analytics briefs, trend drivers, caveats, confidence notes, audience-specific outputs, and disclosure-risk markers without turning descriptive trends into unsupported forecast, causal, fee, or policy claims.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: high-volume intake and service execution
workflow_stages.description: Segment urgent portfolio or property intake by freshness, severity, affected channel, property, reporting deadline, customer-impact signal, and missing record state so service speed does not bypass evidence controls.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: policy-aware exception and customer-impact escalation
workflow_stages.description: When analytics reveals refund, fee, accessibility, safety, payment, fraud, loss-prevention, or guest-service implications, package the evidence and route to the designated owner instead of deciding the exception in-lane.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: reservation pace reports, pickup curves, occupancy history, rooms sold, rooms available, room-night counts, ADR, RevPAR, rooms revenue, and booking-window data
PMS reservation records, CRS records, booking engine reports, channel-manager exports, OTA reports, direct-booking reports, and BI dashboards
cancellation patterns, cancellation reason codes, cancellation deadline rules, no-show trends, no-show disposition notes, lead-time data, source-market segmentation, and channel mix
rate-plan, package, promotion, deposit, guarantee, refund status, payment dispute, duplicate booking, and chargeback flags
metric dictionaries, dashboard definitions, source-precedence rules, reporting cadence, audience definitions, approval matrices, and disclosure-review notes
guest-impact signals, accessibility-sensitive reservation records, suspicious booking or payment patterns, safety or outbreak references, and escalation-owner maps

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source hierarchy, metric definitions, retrieval hooks, and reporting context
prepare evidence-backed reservation analytics briefs, metric definitions, caveat notes, reconciliation notes, exception notes, and handoff packages without overstating authority
determine which metrics, definitions, source records, refresh assumptions, audience, and cadence support the requested analysis
escalate: pricing, rate, promotion, inventory-control, overbooking, or revenue-management approval
refund, cancellation-fee waiver, service-recovery compensation, or policy exception
guest-service resolution, accessible-room exception, payment dispute, fraud investigation, loss-prevention action, or safety/security response
legal interpretation, consumer-protection interpretation, final external disclosure, or public benchmark signoff
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, source hierarchy, or metric definitions are unresolved
override pricing, refund, fee, accessibility, safety, fraud, or guest-service policy without explicit authority
state unsupported causal, forecast, benchmark, public-disclosure, or policy claims
resolve safety, fraud, payment-card, billing-dispute, or customer-harm cases from analytics outputs alone

domain_failure_modes: PMS, CRS, booking engine, channel-manager, and BI conflicts are collapsed into one published metric without source lineage or correction owner
stay-date, booking-date, arrival-date, room-night, rooms-sold, and rooms-available definitions are mixed and produce misleading occupancy, pace, or pickup results
analytics output implies forecast certainty or causality from descriptive pace, cancellation, no-show, or channel trends
mandatory fees, rate-plan rules, cancellation deadlines, refund terms, or accessibility-sensitive booking obligations are ignored when analytics is used for customer-impact decisions
high-volume portfolio reporting pressure causes stale or incomplete records to be treated as current
fraud, duplicate booking, payment, identity, or loss-prevention signals are described as resolved by analytics rather than escalated
safety or outbreak signals are turned into operational conclusions without safety or public-health owner review
tenant-specific metric adaptations silently replace public or historical baselines and create trend drift
the lane widens into pricing, refund, front-office, guest-service, fraud, loss-prevention, safety, legal, or disclosure authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific lodging fee, refund, tax, and cancellation requirements
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline uses U.S. federal consumer, billing, payment-security, accessibility, and disclosure sources. Tenant jurisdiction, OTA contract, tax, refund, and cancellation-policy details must be supplied at runtime before customer-specific or external-facing claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific metric dictionaries and source precedence
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public STR and USALI sources anchor terminology, but tenant PMS, CRS, channel-manager, and BI precedence rules determine final internal reporting. The pack blocks confident reporting until those rules are available for the request.

The lane can explain descriptive pace and historical trends, but forecast models, confidence intervals, external benchmark use, and publication signoff require tenant methodology and approval context.

notes: Authoritative reservation analytics records are tenant-designated PMS analytics, PMS reservation records, CRS, booking engine, channel manager, OTA or direct-booking reports, BI, payment, CRM, fraud, and incident systems constrained by the public sources above.
The lane stops before pricing, refund, safety, fraud, guest-service, front-office, legal, and final disclosure decisions even when the analytical signal is urgent.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec-pack because all open items are explicitly represented as runtime retrieval dependencies and refusal conditions.
